featured.description: White-label engine for digital asset marketplaces. A single, scalable architecture that accelerates time-to-market, proven in production by over seven clients across radically different industries.

items.description: Blockchain-anchored document certification and traceability, built for real-world adoption by non-technical teams.
Secure case management for notaries, built for sensitive exchanges without adding friction to daily operations.
Wealth management platform for IFAs and Middle Office, built for two profiles with distinct operational workflows.

about.label: About

skills.title: Product & UX
skills.description: End-to-end interface design that plans for edge cases and errors, keeping production stable.

skills.title: Design Systems
skills.description: A structured, adaptable, and documented system that scales evenly across every new feature.

skills.title: Design to Code
skills.description: A seamless transition right into development, so design decisions hold up through production.

body: I design web and product interfaces for highly complex environments, such as fintech, wealth management, and security.

My dual background in product design and front-end development allows me to integrate technical constraints from day one, delivering production-ready assets that ensure a zero-loss production handoff.

process.label: Process
process.heading: How I work
process.description: Four key steps to align product vision, design, and development.

steps.title: Framing
steps.description: Defining the product’s strategic foundations to align business objectives and user expectations from the start.
steps.label: //Step 01

steps.title: Architecture
steps.description: Structuring the product logic prior to building any screens to directly align design components and the codebase.
steps.label: //Step 02

steps.title: Alignment
steps.description: Designing and iterating in short cycles, confronting every decision against product priorities and code realities.
steps.label: //Step 03

steps.title: Handoff
steps.description: Delivering directly actionable specifications and layouts for production, leaving no room for interpretation.
steps.label: //Step 04

contact.label: Contact
contact.heading: Let’s build your project
contact.description: Describe your project in a few words, I’ll get back to you quickly.

form.briefPlaceholder: What is the primary objective of your product and your current constraints (timeline, tech stack, design system...)?

errors.briefRequired: Describe your project in a few words.

intro.description: A B2B financial distribution SaaS designed for two distinct operational profiles. A single interface adapts workflows for IFAs and Middle Office teams while unifying subscription tracking.

context.label: Context
context.heading: One shared dataset across two roles
context.p1: Wenimmo is a B2B financial distribution SaaS. IFAs initiate subscriptions and manage client relationships, while Middle Office teams oversee compliance and case validation.
context.p2: The challenge: merge these divergent workflows into a single tool without adding cognitive friction.

problem.label: Problem
problem.heading: Balancing speed and rigor
problem.p1: IFAs operate on the move, between client meetings. Their priority is speed: track cases and launch subscriptions in just a few clicks. Conversely, Middle Office teams handle high volume and require density: advanced filters, granular pipelines, and thorough administrative tracking.
problem.p2: The challenge lies in building a single system that shifts its UI priorities based on the connected profile, without maintaining two separate products.

solution.label: Solution
solution.heading: One platform, two distinct workflows
solution.p1: The platform relies on a single dataset to guarantee full synchronization. The interface dynamically adapts to each profile by adjusting its visual density, priorities, and actions.
solution.p2: On one side, a linear flow guides the IFA straight to signature. On the other, a dense pipeline is optimized for high-volume Middle Office processing.

features.title: Tailored experience
features.description: The application loads a dedicated environment based on the account type, adapting to each role’s workflow without visual clutter.

features.title: Synchronized flows
features.description: Every action reflects instantly across account types, providing a seamless shared baseline for both roles in real time.

features.title: Invisible compliance
features.description: KYC verification and e-signatures were integrated directly into the funnel and run in the background to preserve flow.

cgp.label: IFA Profile
cgp.heading:
In client meetings,
between consultations
cgp.description: Designed for mobility, the interface enables IFAs to check file statuses instantly, initiate subscriptions, or validate documents on the go. The system preserves context across sessions, allowing users to resume interrupted workflows without any setup.

features.title: Workflow separation
features.description: Investor status (individual/entity) is set at entry to direct the flow and eliminate input errors early on.

features.title: Clear progress tracking
features.description: The stepper makes progress predictable across dense flows, reducing cognitive load and simplifying context recovery.

features.title: Business-oriented statuses
features.description: Labels translate technical logic into business terms, serving operational teams over database logic.

features.title: Irreversible actions
features.description: RTO agreements and final submissions carry legal weight. The UI isolates these steps to highlight their compliance impact.

middleOffice.label: Middle Office Profile
middleOffice.heading: Bulk processing, without losing track
middleOffice.description: The Middle Office daily orchestrates dozens of complex subscriptions with constantly shifting statuses. The interface adapts in real time to streamline mass workflows, automate repetitive tasks, and eliminate the risk of operational errors.

features.title: Centralized view
features.description: The list view consolidates all files to manage every workflow in one place, regardless of the originating IFA.

features.title: Contextual actions
features.description: Whether reminding, flagging, or approving, actions adapt to file status to prevent invalid operations.

features.title: Volume management
features.description: Advanced filters and batch selection speed up mass processing without compromising individual file traceability.

features.title: Confirmed closure
features.description: Once approved by the operator, this final status freezes the visual state and permanently locks the subscription history.

impact.label: Impact
impact.heading: Unmatched productivity
impact.description: Unifying the flow removes the barrier between front and back-office operations. A single interface now orchestrates two distinct roles, ensuring total operational efficiency across teams.

features.title: For IFAs
features.description: Workflow interruptions no longer disrupt operations. Visual tracking makes it seamless to resume any active file, permanently eliminating data entry friction.

features.title: For the Middle Office
features.description: Security and efficiency drive mass operations. Contextual actions and built-in audit trails eliminate manual entry errors and drastically accelerate approvals.

intro.description: A B2B2C notary certification SaaS built for sensitive document exchanges. Its interface aligns notary rigor requirements with the clarity their clients need.

context.label: Context
context.heading: Simple, secure notary management
context.p1: The platform centralizes and automates sensitive document collection. From their workspace, the notary reviews every document before securing the final file via blockchain anchoring.
context.p2: On the client side, a guided workflow allows users to submit credentials and track file status autonomously.

problem.label: Problem
problem.heading: Client clarity, notary control
problem.p1: Notarial document collection traditionally suffers from manual follow-ups, scattered files, and a lack of formal tracking. Faced with this complexity, designing a single shared portal would have required too many compromises.
problem.p2: Notaries demand high data density and total control, while clients require absolute clarity. Merging these asymmetric needs would have inevitably degraded the experience for one side or the other.

solution.label: Solution
solution.heading: Three spaces for a single file
solution.p1: Three distinct spaces rely on a single source of truth, enabling the notary to manage, the client to submit, and the administration to supervise access.
solution.p2: This separation guarantees strict functional isolation. By eliminating broad compromises, each environment is tailored to its user’s priorities without ever degrading the experience for others.

features.title: Separated spaces
features.description: The notary gets a data-dense view built for expertise, while the client benefits from a clean, distraction-free flow.

features.title: Progressive statuses
features.description: A visual pipeline replaces vague states with clear milestones, cutting down on manual client follow-ups.

features.title: Embedded blockchain
features.description: Blockchain anchoring integrates directly into the notary’s workflow, turning into a seamless action with instant visual feedback.

notaire.label: Notary workspace
notaire.heading: Centralizing file management
notaire.p1: The notary workspace centralizes the entire lifecycle of the file, from document requests to final certification. Built as the platform’s most data-dense environment, it enables one-click document validation and blockchain anchoring.
notaire.p2: Management relies on a dynamic status system (empty, in progress, completed, action required, validated, anchored) reflecting the exact status of the file in real time.

client.label: Client workspace
client.heading: Streamlining the interface to guide submission
client.p1: The client environment eliminates cognitive load to focus solely on the required action: knowing which document to submit and where, without the complexity of the management system.
client.p2: Following secure identity verification, a guided flow takes over. The interface clearly separates validated documents from those still requested by the notary office, offering immediate clarity on pending tasks.

admin.label: Admin workspace
admin.heading: Vote-based governance
admin.p1: The admin workspace centralizes control across notary offices using three distinct permission tiers, designed for granular responsibility segmentation.
admin.p2: To protect sensitive data, the Super-Admin role incorporates mandatory voting. This design decision ensures that no critical updates to ecosystem permissions can be unilaterally approved by a single user.

impact.label: Impact
impact.heading: Zero onboarding, zero follow-ups
impact.description: The interface eliminates adoption friction for users with asymmetric workflows. By isolating workspaces, every stakeholder gets an environment tailored to their operational goals, free of visual noise.

features.title: For notary offices
features.description: Zero manual follow-ups lost in email threads. The interface centralizes real-time case tracking, provides full visibility, and enables one-click reminders or certifications.

features.title: For clients
features.description: Full autonomy from day one. Secure identity verification eliminates authentication friction, while the guided flow specifies required documents and notifies the client at every step.

intro.description: A B2B blockchain certification SaaS built for non-technical teams. Its dual-layer architecture provides direct access to cryptographic proofs without cluttering the primary interface.

context.label: Context
context.heading: Making blockchain proof accessible
context.p1: The platform generates cryptographic hashes for files to immutably prove their existence and integrity.
context.p2: The main design challenge was making this technical anchoring seamless for business teams (legal, HR, compliance) who regularly upload and verify sensitive documents.

problem.label: Problem
problem.heading: The blockchain, reliable but unreadable
problem.p1: Raw cryptographic data remains indecipherable for business teams, even though it is essential for technical roles.
problem.p2: The challenge was handling this asymmetry: how to confirm file integrity for a non-technical user without obscuring or altering the raw proof required by developers?

solution.label: Solution
solution.heading: Translating the technical into business language
solution.p1: The solution structures this dual perspective around four core workflows: management via the dashboard, traceability through history, action through deposits, and clarity via verification.
solution.p2: This approach prioritizes information to display the essentials upfront while making technical data accessible on demand. Interface statuses are named to answer a concrete user question rather than describe a system state.

decisions.label: Decisions
decisions.heading: Guiding the business focus
decisions.description: Every display choice was driven by a strict information hierarchy. The experience relies on three major trade-offs, designed to guide the business user’s gaze without ever cluttering the interface.

features.title: Hiding the complexity
features.description: Cryptographic data is removed from the primary view. The interface focuses on a single question: was the deposit successful?

features.title: Batching by group and workspace
features.description: Multiple files deposited together form a single entry in the history. Workspaces then segment them by business context.

features.title: Two levels of detail
features.description: Business teams follow the primary flow. Technical profiles access raw data via a drawer, without cluttering the view.

dashboard.label: Dashboard
dashboard.heading: Simplifying data visualization
dashboard.description: The dashboard centralizes volume metrics and flow statuses to immediately isolate certification anomalies. Designed for operational management rather than mere consultation, this space highlights urgent actions and anchoring failures. The interface protects the user from cognitive overload while keeping the full history available at the second level of detail.

deposits.label: Deposits
deposits.heading: Clarifying deposit tracking
deposits.description: To absorb high volumes of documents, the history segments activity by thematic contexts. The primary view prioritizes a quick read of file statuses, while a side panel centralizes technical tracking and anchoring proofs, accessible on demand.

items.title: Securing certification submissions
items.description: The deposit flow processes multiple files simultaneously. The interface displays a real-time wait time estimation and generates a pre-filled support ticket if a blockage occurs.

items.title: Making verification instant
items.description: This flow simplifies access to blockchain data. The application instantly analyzes the document to deliver a clear visual status, allowing users to verify its authenticity at a glance without dealing with technical complexity.

impact.label: Impact
impact.heading: Optimizing operational efficiency
impact.description: By eliminating friction from raw technical data, the interface removes historical time loss in certification. Information adapts to the user’s role to deliver the exact level of detail required, ensuring complete autonomy for both business teams and developers.

features.title: Business teams
features.description: No need to ask an expert to validate a deposit. Statuses are clear without blockchain knowledge, progress displays in real time, and support remains directly accessible in the interface.

features.title: Technical teams
features.description: No need to hunt for a hash: everything is centralized within the deposit details. The CSV export covers audit needs, boosting user autonomy to drastically reduce tickets.

intro.description: A white-label Web3 marketplace built on a modular architecture. Designed to adapt to each client, the interface reconfigures instantly without code changes.

context.label: Context
context.heading: Aligning design and code
context.p1: Deploying a single architecture across radically different identities exposes the platform to constant technical debt. Without a strict semantic structure, each client customization threatens global product maintenance.
context.p2: To absorb contrasting worlds, from gaming to finance, the system relies on dynamic theming. Design and code share the exact same variables, eliminating back-and-forth adjustments and ensuring interface integrity with every deployment.

problem.label: Problem
problem.heading: The technical cost of custom builds
problem.p1: What varies from client to client is strictly limited to surface values: colors, typography, border radius, or spacing. Behaviors, hierarchy, and accessibility remain unchanged.
problem.p2: Isolating these two layers makes it possible to deploy dozens of identities without impacting component structures. More than a visual optimization, this is the foundational technical decision that makes white-label viable at scale.

solution.label: Solution
solution.heading: A single system for all brands
solution.p1: To industrialize the product from a single codebase, the solution relies on a three-tier architecture: fixed primitive values, semantic tokens configurable per client, and agnostic components.
solution.p2: Technical integration happens only once, allowing the UI to adapt dynamically. What used to take months of R&D now integrates for a new client in a single week.

decision.label: Decision
decision.heading: Industrializing multi-brand
decision.description: Bloom started without a semantic layer for pragmatic reasons. As the product grew, this technical debt required a three-week refactor to restructure the system. For a scalable product, this intermediate layer is an essential foundation from day one, not a late optimization.

features.title: Raw model limitations
features.description: Binding components directly to raw values speeds up launch, but accumulates technical debt with every client variant.

features.title: The semantic ecosystem
features.description: An intermediate layer translates primitives by context. Changing a client’s identity comes down to a few variables.

architecture.label: Architecture
architecture.heading: Modular components
architecture.description: Some clients impose unexpected constraints. A component that is too rigid forces a fork; too generic, it becomes useless. The answer lies in fixed components with variants built to handle these edge cases.

layers.title: Raw values
layers.description: Each raw value is defined without semantic intent: the technical baseline is set, but remains disconnected from the product.
layers.label: //layer 1

layers.title: Semantic roles
layers.description: Each primitive receives a functional role. Identity is applied here, leaving component architecture completely untouched.
layers.label: //layer 2

layers.title: Final components
layers.description: Components consume only these semantic tokens. Total consistency is preserved, regardless of the client’s identity.
layers.label: //layer 3

alignment.label: Tech alignment
alignment.heading: Designing for prod
alignment.p1: Component states follow CSS pseudo-classes: a token structure like btn-primary-bg-default mirrors the codebase exactly.
alignment.p2: With technical specs embedded directly in Figma, developers implement designs without interpretation. No component is rewritten from scratch: the architecture is self-sustaining.

identities.label: Identities
identities.heading: One structure, multiple identities
identities.p1: Component and token architecture remains identical for everyone. Deploying a new client simply requires injecting its primitives (colors, typography, border radius) via a config file.
identities.p2: The identity propagates instantly across the entire platform. Code-wise, developers apply the theme in a single line without touching components.

method.label: Method
method.heading:
Designed solo,
built to be taken over
method.p1: Working solo demands absolute documentation rigor: naming, justifying, and annotating every decision to ensure smooth production integration.
method.p2: The system must be understood without opening Figma. Components are structured by usage state, and tokens use semantic names over raw values.

features.title: A standalone deliverable
features.description: The deliverable requires no meeting to be explained. Embedded design annotations capture true intent, eliminating post-handoff corrections.

features.title: Code-aligned naming
features.description: Design system and codebase share identical naming conventions. Engineers find their bearings instantly, turning handoff into a simple sync.

items.title: Configuring asset issuance
items.description: From 1-of-1s to full collections, creators configure digital assets in a few clicks. Supply, price, and rarity are defined through a seamless flow that completely abstracts blockchain complexity.

items.title: Streamlining wallet checkout
items.description: A three-step checkout flow: product page, payment, and secure transfer. Every interaction is designed to reassure users and remove friction from digital transactions.

playground.label: Playground
playground.heading: The design system in action
playground.lead: Select a brand to watch the entire system reconfigure.

meta.home: Web and product interface design (fintech, SaaS, marketing sites). Design & front-end rigor for Figma files ready to implement, with no loss in production.
meta.bloom: Lead Product Designer on a white-label Web3 marketplace. Modular architecture, multi-client design system, production delivery.
meta.keepro: Product Designer on a B2B blockchain certification SaaS. Cryptographic proof made readable for non-technical teams.
meta.lecoffre: Product Designer on a B2B2C notary platform. Three distinct spaces, zero manual follow-up, integrated blockchain anchoring.
